dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I thought gathering evidence would be the hard part. I was wrong.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The garrison commander found out we're building a case. They sent soldiers to {location} to 'reinforce' us: but really to intimidate. {target_npc} was taken in for questioning and held for hours. They released them, but the message was clear: drop it or face consequences. Worse, the soldiers confiscated half the supplies you gathered, calling it 'back taxes.' I need you to retrieve more {quest_item} and find a way to deliver our evidence to the regional council through a route the garrison doesn't control. There's an old messenger trail {location_hint}, but {enemy_type} have been spotted along it.
dialogueChunks.outro: They broke their oath and now they're threatening us for noticing. The only thing more dangerous than a liar is a liar with soldiers.

playerResponses.text: Intimidation won't work when the truth gets out. I'll find a way through.
Soldiers, monsters, and a race against time. Just another delivery job.
They have soldiers and we have documents. This fight is over.

dialogueChunks.intro: They had the nerve to come back here, {player_name}. Right through the front door, smiling.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} returned to the tavern acting as if nothing happened. Charm, apologies, talk of fresh starts. But I found new ledgers hidden in the back room: they never stopped the side deals. They've been selling to {enemy_type} traders this entire time, and now the contraband trail leads to a warehouse {location_hint}. I need you to go there and document what you find: crates, manifests, anything that proves the scope of the betrayal. And I need more {quest_item} to keep the tavern running while I prepare my case for the council.
dialogueChunks.outro: They looked me in the eye and lied. Again. No more chances. No more benefit of the doubt. This ends at the council table.

playerResponses.text: The warehouse will tell the whole story. I'll go.
Coming back and lying to your face. That takes a special kind of rotten. I'll get your evidence.
Partner disputes should stay between partners. I'm not a detective.

dialogueChunks.intro: The guild master moved faster than we expected, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The original covenant has been moved from the guild hall. {target_npc} watched guards carry sealed chests out the back door last night. They're destroying evidence. The ledgers I have are damning, but without the original signed covenant showing exactly what was promised, it's my word against the guild master's. There's a clerk who kept copies of every guild document in a private archive {location_hint}. The {enemy_type} enforcers are watching the roads, but if you can reach that archive and bring back the copy, we have an airtight case.
dialogueChunks.outro: Destroying a signed covenant is a crime in itself. If we prove they did it, there's nowhere left to hide.

playerResponses.text: Destroying evidence means they're scared. I'll find the copy.
Enforcers on the roads and evidence vanishing. We need to move fast.
If they're destroying records, they've already won. Walk away.
